Kiriakou described the incident as an example of Israeli military conduct in the occupied territories, stating that Abu Akleh was clearly identified as press when she was killed with a sniper. articles.summary: Reports say Pentagon raised Israeli espionage threat level to ‘critical’ The Pentagon’s Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA) has reportedly raised its assessment of the threat posed by Israeli espionage from “high” to “critical”, according to reports by NBC News and The New York Times, which cited anonymous US officials. The reports said the change was driven by concerns that Israel has adopted increasingly aggressive intelligence-gathering tactics amid tensions surrounding the US-Israel war on Iran. articles.summary: Hundreds march in Manchester in support of Palestinians Hundreds of people carrying Palestinian flags marched through the streets of Manchester to express solidarity with Palestinians and call on the British government to end its support for Israel. Videos shared by Friends of Palestine in Greater Manchester showed demonstrators carrying flags, chanting pro-Palestinian slogans and displaying a large banner reading “End the Israeli Occupation”. The event took place at the main entrance of Scotland’s national stadium while Scotland’s women’s national team played Israel in Hungary behind closed doors. Video released by the group showed participants laying wreaths and displaying football shirts and Palestinian scarves in tribute to the Palestinian players who were killed. articles.summary: Jordan condemns Israeli attack on Lebanese military convoy Jordan’s Ministry of Foreign and Expatriate Affairs called for an immediate end to Israel’s attacks on Lebanon and described the deadly strike on a Lebanese military convoy as “a blatant violation of the sovereignty, security, and stability” of the country. Foreign ministry spokesperson Fouad Majali expressed condolences to the Lebanese government and the families of those killed, while reaffirming Jordan’s “unwavering support” for Lebanon, according to the state-run Petra news agency. articles.title: Report says US weighing use of frozen Iranian assets for Gulf reconstruction
articles.summary: Report says US weighing use of frozen Iranian assets for Gulf reconstruction US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is considering whether frozen Iranian assets could be redirected to help Gulf states rebuild and repair damage caused by Iran, according to a report by Reuters citing a source familiar with the matter. The source said Bessent has asked Treasury officials to assess the extent of damage already sustained by US allies in the Gulf and to examine whether Iranian assets could be used to fund future reconstruction and repair efforts. articles.title: US senator criticises plan linking Israeli and US defence industries
articles.summary: US senator criticises plan linking Israeli and US defence industries US Senator Bernie Sanders said he would oppose legislation under consideration in Congress that he argues would further integrate Israel into the US military-industrial system through defence co-production arrangements. In a post on X, Sanders accused Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of attempting to shift military assistance into Pentagon-linked programmes rather than reducing aid. “Netanyahu knows Americans are sick of funding Israel’s wars. articles.title: Centcom says US forces shot down Iranian drones near Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Centcom says US forces shot down Iranian drones near Strait of Hormuz US Central Command (Centcom) said American forces shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones that it said posed a threat to international maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. In a statement, Centcom said the drones were intercepted before they could threaten shipping in the strategic waterway. The command added that US forces remain “postured and ready” to continue defending against what it described as Iranian aggression in the region. Earlier today, U.S. forces in the Middle East shot down two Iranian one-way attack

articles.title: US Ambassador to UN vows to dismantle structures seen as anti-Israel
articles.summary: US Ambassador to UN vows to dismantle structures seen as anti-Israel US Ambassador to the United Nations Jeff Bartos said Washington’s efforts to reform the United Nations are partly aimed at addressing what he described as longstanding bias against the United States and Israel within the international body. In a post on X, Bartos said the Trump administration had already achieved major reforms, including the elimination of 3,000 bureaucratic positions and a reduction of $570 million from the UN budget this year. articles.title: Doha calls strike on Lebanese army patrol a ‘dangerous escalation’
articles.summary: Doha calls strike on Lebanese army patrol a ‘dangerous escalation’ Qatar’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned the Israeli attack on a Lebanese army patrol that killed two officers and a soldier, describing it as a “dangerous escalation and a blatant violation of the sovereignty” of Lebanon. In a statement, the ministry called on the international community to compel Israel to halt what it described as repeated attacks on Lebanon, respect international law and fully implement UN Security Council Resolution 1701. Qatar also reaffirmed its support for Lebanon’s unity, sovereignty, and territor

articles.title: Saudi Arabia rejects Israel's 'violation of Lebanon’s sovereignty'
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia rejects Israel's 'violation of Lebanon’s sovereignty' Saudi Arabia’s foreign ministry condemned what it described as the continued Israeli aggression against Lebanon, expressing its “condemnation and denunciation in the strongest terms” of Israel’s actions. In a statement, the ministry reaffirmed Saudi Arabia’s rejection of attacks on the Lebanese Armed Forces and violations of Lebanon’s sovereignty. It also offered condolences to the families of Lebanese military personnel who were killed while carrying out what it described as their national duties.

Naqvi said he had delivered 'an important' message from Field Marshal General Asim Munir, Pakistan’s army chief and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif to Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, concerning the regional situation. articles.summary: Hezbollah says it carried out 22 attacks on Israeli forces in south Lebanon Hezbollah said it carried out 22 attacks against Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Saturday, targeting soldiers, tanks and military positions with attack drones, rocket barrages and artillery fire. articles.title: Pope Leo says war on Iran fails Catholic ‘just war’ criteria
articles.summary: Pope Leo says war on Iran fails Catholic ‘just war’ criteria Pope Leo said the US-Israel war on Iran does not meet the standards of a “just war” under Catholic teaching, reiterating a position he has previously expressed during a visit to Madrid. Responding to reporters, the head of the Catholic Church said: “In Iran, the criteria for a just war are not present.” He added that traditional just war theory was developed in an era when humanity could not imagine the destructive power of modern weapons. articles.title: How Bolivia's Plans to Tap Mineral Riches Are Being Tested by Mass Unrest
articles.summary: Bolivia’s new president, Rodrigo Paz, is attempting to rebrand the country as a destination for foreign investment after two decades of socialist rule. Sitting atop some of the world’s largest lithium resources and vast mineral reserves, Bolivia has long struggled to translate its natural resources into economic prosperity. Paz argues that clearer laws, stability, and closer ties with international investors can unlock growth. But, just six months into President Paz’s term, his hopes are meeting the reality of unrest at home and violent protests. (Source: Bloomberg)
